syntax: bf(y ~ x, sigma ~ z), family = gaussian(), engine = "julia"
claim_boundary: Uses the default DRM.jl fitting path; no Julia-side engine_control surface is exposed from R. Covered is scoped to point, logLik, coefficient, and Wald CI-endpoint parity only (engine-vs-engine agreement, not interval coverage); profile/bootstrap bridge intervals for fixed effects stay gated because the Julia bridge profile/bootstrap path supports only phylogenetic SD targets. The aggregate R-Julia bridge gate row stays partial.
next_action: Point/logLik/coefficient parity plus Wald CI-endpoint parity are banked: engine='julia' == engine='tmb' Wald CI endpoints for all four location-scale fixed-effect coefficients to an asserted <= 1e-4 (measured ~5.6e-6); tests/testthat/test-julia-tmb-parity.R 'Route C interval parity', callr-isolated. Keep parity tests tied to exact bridge payloads; do not extend covered to profile/bootstrap fixed-effect intervals or to other routes without their own per-cell parity.

syntax: bf(mu1 = y1 ~ x, mu2 = y2 ~ x, sigma1 = ~1, sigma2 = ~1, rho12 = ~x), family = biv_gaussian(), engine = "julia"
drmjl_status: default DRM.jl bivariate Gaussian residual-correlation path
claim_boundary: Fixed-effect non-phylogenetic residual rho12 ~ x only (biv_gaussian); covered is point, logLik, coefficient, and Wald CI-endpoint parity (engine vs engine, not interval coverage). Phylogenetic rho12 (biv_rho12_phylo gate), cross-family rho12, random-effect rho12, and profile/bootstrap bridge intervals stay gated/planned; the aggregate R-Julia bridge gate row and the design-168 matrix bridge cell stay as-is.
next_action: rho12 ~ x bridge parity is banked: engine='julia' == engine='tmb' on all eight fixed-effect coefficients including rho12:(Intercept)/rho12:x and Wald CI endpoints to an asserted <= 1e-4 (measured ~1.3e-6); tests/testthat/test-julia-tmb-parity.R 'Route B lead novelty', callr-isolated. Keep parity tied to exact bridge payloads; do not extend to profile/bootstrap or other rho12 routes without their own per-cell parity.

claim_boundary: Gaussian-only response masks; missing predictors and non-Gaussian response masks remain gated.
next_action: Keep mask tests Gaussian-only until non-Gaussian observed-data likelihoods are audited.

syntax: biv_gaussian() q4 phylo on mu1, mu2, sigma1, sigma2 with REML = TRUE
drmjl_status: q4 PLSM REML path when installed DRM.jl supports it
claim_boundary: Requires the full four-axis phylogenetic location-scale grammar; do not infer native TMB restricted-likelihood support for scale-side structured effects.
next_action: Bank fit-specific CI/status parity before release language.

syntax: poisson()/nbinom2() with phylo(1 | group, tree = tree)
claim_boundary: Large-p phylogenetic random-intercept route only; non-phylogenetic count models stay native TMB.
next_action: Keep non-phylo count bridge errors in the gate registry.

syntax: Gamma()/beta()/stats::binomial() with phylo(1 | group, tree = tree)
claim_boundary: Finite-and-sane bridge smoke evidence only; no native TMB parity or non-phylo binomial bridge promotion.
next_action: Add comparator or parity evidence before promoting beyond experimental.

syntax: relmat(1 | group, K = K) for supported one-response families
drmjl_status: general-covariance path for Gaussian, Poisson, NB2, and Gamma
claim_boundary: Requires covariance/relatedness matrix K and sigma ~ 1; beta, precision Q, and sigma predictors stay gated.
next_action: Compare current DRM.jl accepted families with the R gate before widening.

drmjl_status: latent-rho mixed-family path; API drift is tracked in tests
claim_boundary: Latent-rho development route; public docs must not present rho12 formulas or release-ready cross-family inference.
next_action: Resolve the mixed-family API mismatch before any public promotion.

syntax: engine_control = ... or non-default Julia optimizer controls
claim_boundary: Do not document user-selectable Julia optimizer controls until a real R API is designed.
next_action: Design engine_control explicitly before relaxing the gate.

drmjl_status: direct Binomial evidence is not an R non-phylo bridge claim
claim_boundary: Native TMB #569 owns ordinary binomial support; Julia bridge binomial remains separate evidence.
next_action: Wait for #569 native parity plus a separate bridge parity PR.

syntax: confint(fit, parm = "mu:x", method = "profile" | "bootstrap") on bf(y ~ x + phylo(1 | sp, tree = tree), sigma ~ 1), family = gaussian(), engine = "julia"
drmjl_status: in-process profile_result coefficient targets via drm_bridge_inference profile_param/profile_coef passthrough
claim_boundary: Stage A/B (design 179): a SINGLE fixed-effect MU coefficient of a Gaussian phylo model via the single-row bridge path. partial = engine agreement, NOT interval coverage. PROFILE: engine=julia profile endpoints match native tmbprofile to the asserted test tolerance 1e-3 (measured ~2e-5, one seed-fixed n_tip=40 fixture). BOOTSTRAP: cold parametric bootstrap row for the coefficient -- feasibility + sanity (finite CI brackets the estimate), stochastic, NOT tight parity. SIGMA coefficient profiles are NOT offered (DRM.jl parm=:sigma diverges at the log-sigma boundary). Multi-coefficient batching, sigma/scale targets, and warm-start bootstrap are NOT reachable; native R/TMB / direct DRM.jl / Julia-via-R lanes stay separate.
next_action: Land multi-coefficient batching and boundary-robust sigma coefficient profiles; warm-start bootstrap (LocScale path) + warm-vs-cold parity gate; then multi-seed/model evidence before any covered promotion.
